title: Lecture Three Chiral Wall-Mode Context
summary: This equation context records the exponentially localized wall ansatz, the gamma_1 chirality projection, and the reduced tangential Dirac equation that together produce the chiral boundary mode.
assumptions: The physical edge mode must be normalizable away from the wall.
scope: Equation-level context for the localized chiral wall mode.

source_anchors.notes: Localized ansatz and reduced boundary Dirac equation.

representation: Equation context for the chiral wall mode supported by the sign-changing mass.

math_expressions.notes: Boundary chirality projection and reduced tangential Dirac equation.

symbols.meaning: boundary-localized spinor after factoring out the wall profile
normal gamma matrix fixing the chirality of the wall mode

retrieval_hints: Use when an answer must reconstruct the actual wall-mode ansatz and chirality condition.
